question_text: Three types of electromagnetic radiation are listed below with their wavelengths. Which correctly shows them in order from shortest to longest wavelength, and therefore from most to least energetic?

options.label: A
options.text: Ultraviolet (100 nm), X-rays (0.1 nm), Gamma rays (0.001 nm)

options.label: B
options.text: Gamma rays (0.001 nm), X-rays (0.1 nm), Ultraviolet (100 nm)

options.label: C
options.text: X-rays (0.1 nm), Ultraviolet (100 nm), Gamma rays (0.001 nm)

options.label: D
options.text: Ultraviolet (100 nm), Gamma rays (0.001 nm), X-rays (0.1 nm)

correct_answer: A) Ultraviolet (100 nm), X-rays (0.1 nm), Gamma rays (0.001 nm)
explanation: Shorter wavelengths mean higher frequencies and therefore higher energy in the electromagnetic spectrum. Gamma rays have the shortest wavelength (0.001 nm), so they are most energetic; ultraviolet has the longest of these three (100 nm), so it is least energetic. This ordering is crucial for understanding the ionising power and penetration ability of different radiations.
